Tests the immutability of Object.prototype properties in JavaScript.

# Purpose
This code is a script that tests the properties of the `Object.prototype` in JavaScript. It sets three variables: `writable`, `enumerable`, and `configurable` to `false` initially. The script then attempts to modify `Object.prototype` by assigning it the string "qwerty" and checks if the assignment was successful to determine if the `writable` property is `true`. It iterates over the properties of `Object` to check if any property is equal to "qwerty", setting `enumerable` to `true` if found. Finally, it tries to delete `Object.prototype` to see if it is `configurable`, setting `configurable` to `true` if the deletion is successful. The script concludes by asserting that none of these properties (`writable`, `enumerable`, `configurable`) are `true`, which verifies the immutability of `Object.prototype`.
